author: nflaig
content: so it's breaking because of lint checks, not when running the tests

author: nflaig
content: the problem is that FCR helpers operate on the phase0 type

author: nflaig

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm your PR adds FCR specs for gloas. Is that intentional?

author: nflaig
content: the lint catches the type mismatch now, it didn't before

author: m.kalinin

content: so, any change will cause linter to fail now? or just changing that type. if this is the latter then i will change the type as a part of FCR change which will enable it for gloas

author: jtraglia
content: I haven't looked into the issue. But no, "any change will cause linter to fail now" is not correct.

author: jtraglia

author: nflaig
content: it's just that `mypy` now properly checks the type, while the ssz container seems to be read as `Any`, once you would run FCR for gloas you would get a runtime error though

content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5218 CI is green now but we can close that, was just to demonstrate the issue
